metadata.title:
The Defense Reveals Part Plnkertons Played In


Helping Miners to Cause Trouble.

pages.raw_text:

road and beat him with revolvers
and a whip. They nearly killed
him.


They told him to get out of the
district and stay out. He has new
err been back since.


Maurice Friedman, formerly at
tached to the Denver Pinkerton
agency, was the next witness. He
was a stenographer for MCFARLAND
during the Cripple Greek strike and
also handled reports Of operatives
in the field. He had an operative
named Crane at Colorado City when
the strike Of smeltermen started.
Crane Joined the union, was chair
man of the strike committee and
made daily reports to the Denver
Office. Crane was finally exposed.


A man named Cabin was then
sent to take his place, but couldn't
get In the union. The PinkertOns
had many operatives in the unions,
and, he swore, had men In both the
Western Federation and United
Mine Workers of America.


An operative named Lundruner,
now assistant Pinkerton superin-
tendent at Denver, he swore, was
sent to Butte to learn the miners
secrets.


James Mooney, a former men,
Der of the national board Of the
Mine Workers union, now a Mis-
souri, coal operator, told Of the
trouble his organization had in
Colorado in 1903 and 1904. Lyle
Gregory did duty there as an agent
for the operators.


Mooney was terribly beaten by
deputies on a train near Trinidad.
He swore he had to go to hospital,
where he remained 6 months.


Lyle Gregory's agency furnished
strikebreakers for the Trinidad
fields. Mooney was beaten up by
Gregory's men.


Chris Evans, Of Indianapolis, or
ganizer for the United Mine Work
ers of America, was the next wit
ness and gave testimony along
similar lines.


BY L. F. Taylor late yesterday
afternoon the defense showed that
the bloodhounds put on the scent
after the independence depot explo-
sion ran first to house occupied by
AL Beemore. Beemore was a dep
uty sheriff recognized as being en-
ployed by the mine owners.


Through Thomas Wood the de-
tense brought out It's strongest wit
ness in support Of the theory that
the indicator explosion was
caused by accident and that this &C.
cident was laid on the miners.
Wood was working in the mine and
had left box of dynamite in the
